before.descriptionShort: Left winger Trevor Hendry, acquired from the Richmond Sockeyes in a trade for Dean Rutledge, scores five goals and adds three assists in his first two games with the Fort McMurray Oil Barons.
before.descriptionLong: The Fort McMurray Oil Barons acquired six-foot-two, 185-pound left winger Trevor Hendry from the BCJHL's Richmond Sockeyes in exchange for Dean Rutledge, the AJHL's third-leading scorer, who chose to finish his junior career in Richmond. In his first two home games against the Hobbema Hawks, Hendry scored five goals and added three assists while playing on a line with 16-year-old centre Brian Shantz and right winger Barry Kress. Hendry, a WHL veteran of three-and-a-half years, said the Barons organization had treated him well despite being far from home on the B.C. coast.
before.headline: Sockeyes' Hendry Makes Splash with Oil Barons

after.descriptionShort: Left winger Trevor Hendry, acquired from the Richmond Sockeyes in a trade for Dean Rutledge, scores five goals and adds three assists in his first two games with the Fort McMurray Oil Barons.
after.descriptionLong: The Fort McMurray Oil Barons acquired six-foot-two, 185-pound left winger Trevor Hendry from the BCJHL's Richmond Sockeyes in exchange for Dean Rutledge, the AJHL's third-leading scorer, who chose to finish his junior career in Richmond. In his first two home games against the Hobbema Hawks, Hendry scored five goals and added three assists while playing on a line with 16-year-old centre Brian Shantz and right winger Barry Kress. Hendry, a WHL veteran of three-and-a-half years, said the Barons organization had treated him well despite being far from home on the B.C. coast.
after.headline: Sockeyes' Hendry Makes Splash with Oil Barons

before.descriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeat Humboldt Broncos 4-3 in overtime in Centennial Cup playoff series.
before.descriptionLong: Tony Bobbitt scored the overtime winner at 1:04 of extra time as the Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 4-3 in a Centennial Cup junior hockey playoff game in Humboldt, Saskatchewan on April 22, 1987. The victory gave Richmond a 2-1 lead in the best-of-seven Tier 2 western final series. Tomlinson, Claringbull, and Clarke also scored for the Sockeyes, who trailed 1-0 after one period despite being outshot 39-23.
before.headline: Sockeyes Win Centennial Cup Playoff Game

after.descriptionShort: Tony Bobbitt's goal 1:04 into a 10-minute overtime gave the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 win over the Humboldt Broncos, putting Richmond up 2-1 in the Centennial Cup Tier 2 western final series.
after.descriptionLong: In a CP wire report, Tony Bobbitt scored 1:04 into a 10-minute overtime period to give the Richmond Sockeyes a 4-3 victory over the Humboldt Broncos in Humboldt, Saskatchewan on April 22, 1987, staking Richmond to a 2-1 lead in the best-of-seven Tier 2 western final series. Dave Tomlinson, Mike Claringbull, and Rob Clarke scored the other Sockeyes goals; Bill McDougall (two) and Curtis Chamberlin replied for the Saskatchewan champion Broncos. Richmond was outshot 39-23, trailed 1-0 after one period and 2-1 after two, and took 13 of 25 minor penalties plus the game's only major. Game 4 was scheduled for Richmond on Saturday.
after.headline: Sockeyes Win Centennial Cup Playoff Game

before.descriptionShort: Matt Hervey scores twice as Richmond Sockeyes defeat Humboldt Broncos 5-3 in Centennial Cup Western final.
before.descriptionLong: Matt Hervey scored two goals to lead the Richmond Sockeyes to a 5-3 victory over the Humboldt Broncos on Sunday night in Centennial Cup playoff action. The win gave Richmond a 3-2 series lead in the best-of-seven Western Canada final. Brian Kozak, Dave Tomlinson, and Dave Rutherford also scored for Richmond, while goalie Frank Romeo made 30 saves.
before.headline: Richmond Sockeyes Down Humboldt 5-3

after.descriptionShort: Matt Hervey scored twice and Frank Romeo made 30 saves as the Richmond Sockeyes defeated the Humboldt Broncos 5-3 Sunday night to take a 3-2 lead in the best-of-seven Centennial Cup Western Canada final.
after.descriptionLong: Matt Hervey scored two goals to lead the Richmond Sockeyes past the Humboldt Broncos 5-3 on Sunday night in Game 5 of the best-of-seven Centennial Cup Western Canada final, giving Richmond a 3-2 series lead. Brian Kozak, Dave Tomlinson, and Dave Rutherford (roster name uncertain — possibly Dean Rutledge per OCR) also scored for Richmond after the Sockeyes erased a 2-0 first-period deficit. Goalie Frank Romeo stopped 30 shots while Bruce Hoffort made 22 saves for Humboldt, whose tight-checking style had beaten Richmond 2-1 the previous night. Game 6 was scheduled for Humboldt on Tuesday.
after.headline: Richmond Sockeyes Down Humboldt 5-3

before.descriptionShort: Career follow-up on Steve Jaques, former Tri-City Americans defenseman and captain, now with Phoenix Roadrunners.
before.descriptionLong: Steve Jaques, who served as captain of the Tri-City Americans during the 1989-90 WHL season, spent the 1990-91 season with the Phoenix Roadrunners of the IHL, a Los Angeles Kings farm team. In 58 regular season games, Jaques recorded 10 goals, 24 assists, and 227 penalty minutes; in nine playoff games he had 2 goals, 6 assists, and 21 penalty minutes before Phoenix was eliminated by Peoria in the Turner Cup semifinals.
before.headline: Steve Jaques

after.descriptionShort: A multi-player follow-up column on ex-Tri-City Americans, including a section on former Sockeyes defenseman Steve Jaques — who with Sockeye Stan Czenczek ("Spenner") formed the "Bruise Brothers" — covering his 1990-91 season with the IHL's Phoenix Roadrunners.
after.descriptionLong: A Tri-City Herald column traces the post-junior careers of several former Americans players, including a dedicated section on Steve Jaques, a defenseman who played for the 1987 Richmond Sockeyes alongside Stan Czenczek ("Spenner") as the famed "Bruise Brothers." Jaques served as captain of the Tri-City Americans during the 1989-90 WHL season before spending 1990-91 with the Phoenix Roadrunners of the IHL, a Los Angeles Kings farm team. In 58 regular-season games he recorded 10 goals, 24 assists, and 227 penalty minutes; in nine playoff games he added 2 goals, 6 assists, and 21 penalty minutes before Phoenix was eliminated by Peoria in the Turner Cup semifinals. A Roadrunners broadcaster noted Jaques "grew more aggressive" and finished the year at plus-7.
after.headline: Steve Jaques

before.descriptionShort: NHL Entry Draft selections from rounds 10-12, June 1989.
before.descriptionLong: The Buffalo News publishes a comprehensive listing of NHL Entry Draft picks from the 10th, 11th, and 12th rounds conducted on June 18, 1989. The selection includes draft order numbers, teams, player names, positions, and their junior or collegiate affiliations. Notable picks include Vladimir Malakhov (New York Islanders), Trevor Buchanan from Kamloops WHL (Hartford), and Steve Jaques from Tri-Cities WHL (Los Angeles).

after.descriptionShort: NHL Entry Draft rounds 10–12 listings from June 18, 1989, including pick #228 Steve Jaques (ex-Richmond Sockeyes) selected by the Los Angeles Kings from Tri-Cities (WHL).
after.descriptionLong: The Buffalo News publishes the full NHL Entry Draft pick listings for rounds 10 through 12, conducted June 18, 1989. Among the 63 selections listed are several notable picks including Vladimir Malakhov (New York Islanders, 191st overall), Artur Irbe (Minnesota, 196th), and Vladimir Konstantinov (Detroit, 221st). Of particular archive relevance, Richmond Sockeyes alumnus Steve Jaques is selected 228th overall by the Los Angeles Kings, listed as playing defense for Tri-Cities (WHL). The page also contains adjacent-column noise from earlier draft rounds (approximately picks 70–102).

before.descriptionShort: Toronto Maple Leafs schedule listing for January through April 1993.
before.descriptionLong: This newspaper clipping from The Toronto Star contains the Toronto Maple Leafs' game schedule spanning from January through April 1993, listing opponents, dates, times, and game locations. The page also includes a brief injury report on Vancouver Canucks forward Greg Adams, who suffered a severe charley horse and was expected to miss four to six weeks of play.

after.descriptionShort: Toronto Star NHL notes item reporting that Maple Leafs rookie forward Dave Tomlinson (ex-Richmond Sockeyes) was expected to rejoin the AHL St. John's Leafs after the team's three-game road trip.
after.descriptionLong: This January 18, 1993 Toronto Star page includes an NHL notes column ("Slapshots") mentioning that rookie forward Dave Tomlinson — an alumnus of the 1987 Centennial Cup-winning Richmond Sockeyes — was expected to return to the AHL St. John's Leafs at the end of the Toronto Maple Leafs' three-game road trip, having been passed on the depth chart by returning defenceman Darryl Shannon. The page also carries the Toronto Maple Leafs' full game schedule from January through April 1993, and a brief injury report on Vancouver Canucks forward Greg Adams, who suffered a severe charley horse and was expected to miss four to six weeks.

The draft accurately describes the Maple Leafs schedule and Greg Adams injury note, but those are secondary to the Tomlinson mention for this archive's purposes.

before.descriptionShort: Toronto Star hockey column covering NHL trades, AHL games, and player anecdotes from November 1992.
before.descriptionLong: This Toronto Star sports section article from November 2, 1992, reports on multiple hockey developments: an NHL trade of defenceman Paul Cavallini from St. Louis to Washington for centre Kevin Miller; a record-setting 10-2 victory by the St. John's Maple Leafs over Hershey in the AHL with Yanic Perrault scoring three goals; and humorous anecdotes about NHL players, including Red Wings defenceman Dennis Vial's tax policy commentary and a Soviet player's experience at Denny's.
before.headline: Maple Leafs

after.descriptionShort: Toronto Star hockey column noting ex-Sockeyes player Dave Tomlinson scored two goals in the St. John's Maple Leafs' record 10-2 AHL win over Hershey, November 2, 1992.
after.descriptionLong: Frank Orr's "Slapshots" column in the November 2, 1992 Toronto Star includes a note on the St. John's Maple Leafs' franchise-record 10-2 AHL win over the Hershey Bears, in which ex-Richmond Sockeyes forward Dave Tomlinson scored two goals; Yanic Perrault led with three. The column also covers the NHL trade of defenceman Paul Cavallini from St. Louis to Washington for Kevin Miller, and humorous anecdotes about Red Wings defenceman Dennis Vial and Soviet rookie Viacheslav Butsayev at Denny's.
after.headline: SLAPSHOTS

before.descriptionShort: Toronto Maple Leafs NHL schedule and brief game notes from October-January season schedule.

Brief notes mention Grant Fuhr's sprained knee, Glenn Anderson's injury examination, and recent performances by players including Dimitri Mironov's two goals and Felix Potvin's first NHL victory in a 5-3 win over Ottawa.

after.descriptionShort: A scouting note in The Toronto Star's Maple Leafs game-day column mentions ex-Richmond Sockeye Dave Tomlinson, playing for the AHL's St. John's Maple Leafs, combining with linemates Patrick Augusta and Todd Hawkins for five goals and 12 points in a 6-2 win over Adirondack.
after.descriptionLong: A brief AHL scouting item in The Toronto Star's October 22, 1992 Maple Leafs game-day column notes that right wing Patrick Augusta recorded two goals and three assists as the St. John's Maple Leafs defeated the Adirondack Red Wings 6-2. Augusta combined with linemates Dave Tomlinson — a former Richmond Sockeye — and Todd Hawkins for five goals and 12 points. The surrounding page also features the Maple Leafs' 1992-93 NHL schedule, game notes on injuries to Grant Fuhr and Glenn Anderson, Dimitri Mironov's two-goal game, and Felix Potvin's first NHL win in a 5-3 victory over Ottawa.
